categories.title: New Orleans Swamp Tours
categories.description: Compare swamp tours, airboat rides, and bayou experiences that pair well with a New Orleans city trip.
categories.intro: Swamp tours are one of the most popular ways visitors add Louisiana landscape and wildlife to a New Orleans stay. They work best as a half-day or day-trip experience, especially if you want a strong contrast to the French Quarter, food, and music parts of the trip.
bullets: Best for first-time visitors who want a classic Louisiana experience beyond the city center
Often easiest to do in the morning or as a dedicated half-day block
Works well alongside food, music, and neighborhood-focused city days
Good fit for families, mixed-age groups, and visitors who want scenery and wildlife

intents.label: Airboat swamp tours
intents.description: Faster-paced rides for visitors who want more action on the water.

intents.label: Bayou tours
intents.description: A slower way to see marsh scenery, wildlife, and surrounding wetlands.

intents.label: Family-friendly swamp tours
intents.query: family friendly swamp tour new orleans
intents.description: Useful for visitors balancing city highlights with easy outdoor time.

intents.label: Half-day swamp tours
intents.query: new orleans half day swamp tour
intents.description: The easiest option for fitting a swamp experience into a short trip.

categories.title: New Orleans Ghost Tours
categories.description: Find ghost tours, haunted history walks, and evening experiences that fit naturally with French Quarter and nighttime plans.
categories.intro: Ghost tours are one of the easiest evening experiences to add to a New Orleans trip. They work especially well for first-time visitors who want history, atmosphere, and a more structured night in the French Quarter.
bullets: Best fit for first-timers planning a French Quarter evening
Useful for visitors who want history and storytelling without building a full nightlife itinerary
Easy to pair with dinner, cocktails, or late-night music nearby
Strong option for short stays because the tours fit naturally into a single evening block

intents.label: French Quarter ghost tours
intents.query: french quarter ghost tour new orleans
intents.description: The most common starting point for a haunted-history night.

intents.label: Haunted history tours
intents.description: A better fit if you want local storytelling with more historical context.

intents.label: Cemetery ghost tours
intents.description: Useful for visitors who want a darker, more lore-driven angle.

intents.label: Night walking tours
intents.query: new orleans night walking ghost tour
intents.description: Good when you want one guided experience instead of improvising your evening.

categories.title: New Orleans Food Tours
categories.description: Discover food tours, tasting walks, and culinary experiences that help visitors go beyond one famous meal stop.
categories.intro: Food tours are one of the easiest ways to understand New Orleans quickly. They combine neighborhoods, local dishes, and cultural context in a format that works well for first-time visits and short trips.
bullets: Best for travelers who want structure instead of guessing where to eat
Easy to pair with the French Quarter, Garden District, or a weekend itinerary
Strong option for first-day orientation because food tours also teach the city
Useful for visitors who want to combine history, walking, and meals in one block

intents.label: French Quarter food tours
intents.query: french quarter food tour new orleans
intents.description: A natural first booking for visitors staying near the Quarter.

intents.label: Creole and Cajun food tours
intents.query: new orleans creole cajun food tour
intents.description: Useful when the dishes themselves are the reason for the trip.

intents.label: Cocktail and food tours
intents.description: A good fit for adults building a food-and-nightlife itinerary.

intents.label: Neighborhood tasting tours
intents.description: Helpful for seeing more than one tourist-heavy corridor.

categories.title: New Orleans Jazz and Music Tours
categories.description: Find music-focused tours, jazz walks, and local experiences that help visitors plan around live performance and neighborhood energy.
categories.intro: Music tours help visitors connect New Orleans history with the city's live sound. They work best for travelers who want more than a random night out and want context before choosing where to go after dark.
bullets: Best fit for music-first trips or visitors planning around Frenchmen Street
Good for travelers who want history, neighborhoods, and live-music context together
Easy to pair with city shows, nightlife, and weekend planning pages
Useful when you want a stronger start to an evening instead of wandering blind

intents.label: Jazz history tours
intents.description: A strong entry point for visitors who want background before live music.

intents.label: Frenchmen Street music tours
intents.query: frenchmen street music tour new orleans
intents.description: Helpful for building a dedicated music-first night.

intents.label: Live music walking tours
intents.query: new orleans live music walking tour
intents.description: Useful when you want local context and venue guidance together.

intents.label: Jazz and nightlife experiences
intents.description: Good for visitors pairing music with an evening social plan.
